# SPEC-007: Memory Distillation

**Status**: INFERRED — generated by /generate-specs on 2026-03-22. Requires human review.
**Category**: core
**Created**: 2026-03-22

**Covers**: `agents/distiller.md`, `commands/memory-distill.md`, `commands/memory-config.md`, `commands/memory-stats.md`

## Overview

The memory compression and lifecycle management layer. Compresses raw tier-0 memories into tier-1 digests and promotes high-signal knowledge to tier-2 core. Includes concurrency control (compare-and-swap locking), configuration management for distillation parameters, and anonymized metrics reporting. The distiller agent (Haiku model) operates exclusively via sqlite3 CLI.

## MUST

### Tier Access Control
- MUST NOT allow regular agents to set tier 1 or 2 (only @distiller may set tier > 0; host-script exception: `/init-team` seed import via `import-seed-pack.sh` writes tier-1 digests per SPEC-024 M5)
- MUST check distillation threshold after each memory write when `distill_enabled=true` (skip check entirely when false)

### Distiller Agent
- MUST group tier-0 memories by topic/theme before digesting
- MUST write concise digests (3-8 sentences) preserving: key facts, decisions, patterns, anti-patterns, specific technical details (file paths, function names, gotchas)
- MUST drop from digests: timestamps, transient status, duplicates
- MUST INSERT each digest as tier-1 with source memory IDs in `distilled_from` field
- MUST archive source memories after digesting (set `archived=TRUE`)
- MUST log all tier transitions to distillation_log table
- MUST NEVER delete memories (archive only)
- MUST operate exclusively via sqlite3 CLI (no file reads/writes)
- MUST follow the SPEC-004 write-path contract on every write operation (incl. `PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000`) — SPEC-004 is the single source
- MUST SQL-escape all content (single quotes → double single quotes)
- MUST skip failed batches and continue with remaining

### Tier Promotion (1 → 2)
- MUST promote if: referenced multiple times across sessions, records lesson from mistake, captures key architectural decision, contains permanent domain knowledge
- MUST NOT promote if: routine codebase mapping (file locations), situational context (sprint status, in-progress work), likely to become stale
- MUST UPDATE in-place when promoting (set `tier=2`, `type='core'`)

### Distill Command Orchestration
- MUST require SQLite DB (error if missing)
- MUST use compare-and-swap lock (atomic `UPDATE + changes()` in single sqlite3 session)
- MUST prevent concurrent distillation (skip with error if already locked)
- MUST support `--force` flag to clear stale locks
- MUST release lock in all code paths (success, error, early exit)
- MUST batch memories oldest-first for distillation
- MUST bypass `distill_enabled=false` on manual trigger with `--agent` flag
- MUST support `--status` flag to show tier breakdown without running distillation

### Configuration (memory-config)
- MUST support settable keys: `distill_enabled` (true/false), `distill_mode` (manual/suggest/auto), `distill_threshold` (integer 1-9999), `distill_model` (model name)
- MUST reject writes to protected keys: `distilling_lock`, `schema_version`
- MUST validate values strictly per key (no loose coercion)
- MUST require DB existence (error if missing)

### Statistics (memory-stats)
- MUST never display memory content (only counts, sizes, dates)
- MUST be safe to share publicly (no sensitive data)
- MUST report per-agent breakdown: total memories, type counts, avg/max char length
- MUST report embedding status: mode, model, embedded count vs total
- MUST flag session-start load as HIGH if agent loads >10K chars at session start, moderate if >5K

## SHOULD

- SHOULD respect `distill_model` config when spawning distiller agent (default: haiku)
- SHOULD batch distillation in threshold-sized chunks for manageability
- SHOULD print summary per agent after distillation: `@<agent>: N raw -> M digests | P promoted to core`

## Test

- Verify tier access control: regular agent INSERT with tier=1 is rejected or overridden to tier=0
- Verify distiller groups memories by topic before digesting
- Verify digests preserve technical details (file paths, function names)
- Verify compare-and-swap lock prevents concurrent distillation
- Verify lock is released on error paths
- Verify memory-config rejects protected keys
- Verify memory-stats never exposes memory content

## Validation

- [ ] After distillation: tier-0 source memories have `archived=TRUE`
- [ ] After distillation: tier-1 digests exist with `distilled_from` referencing source IDs
- [ ] Promoted memories have `tier=2, type='core'`
- [ ] `memory-stats` output contains no memory text content
- [ ] Lock cleared after successful distillation

## Open Questions

- [ ] Should `distill_mode: auto` trigger distillation automatically after every N writes, or only at wrap-ticket time?
- [ ] Is the 10K char boot-load warning threshold appropriate, or should it be configurable?
- [ ] Should the distiller be allowed to merge multiple tier-1 digests into a single consolidated tier-1 when topics overlap?
